## Morgan Stanley says software sentiment has turned excessively negative; flags stock picks

A named sell-side call on excessive software pessimism is a potential sentiment inflection point; for a tech PM with software exposure, the specific picks (not disclosed in the headline) are worth obtaining — if they include portfolio names, the upgrade cycle could provide near-term upside.

- Morgan Stanley analysts characterize current software sector sentiment as too negative.
- Specific stock picks are referenced in coverage but not named in the headline.
- Enterprise AI adoption is the relevant thematic lens per classifier tags.
- A named sell-side firm calling a sentiment inflection is a potential sector-rotation signal.
